title: selliguea glauca
intro: Selliguea glauca, also known as Polypodium glauca, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to various parts of the world, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. Selliguea glauca has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including tea, infusion, and tincture, and is often used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
